Feature Audit - Experiment 3 Candidates

Run 1  ·  Generated 2026-04-08 11:47

Purpose: Identify which candidate features (not yet in Experiment 2 Sub-2) are worth adding for Experiment 3, balancing predictive signal against the row-loss cost of including them.

Two missingness metrics are reported - they answer different questions:
  • Pairwise miss% - what fraction of target rows have this feature missing? Measures raw data coverage for the feature in isolation.
  • Marginal cost% - how many additional rows are lost when this feature is added to the existing Experiment 2 Sub-2 dropna? This is the operationally relevant number: if Exp2 already filters out rows due to other missing columns, a new feature with correlated missingness may cost far fewer rows than its raw pairwise % suggests.
Tier logic uses marginal cost (not pairwise miss%) and MI as the signal measure:
AddConsiderLow prioritySkipRedundant - drop
  • Add - MI ≥ 0.20 and marginal cost ≤ 20%
  • Consider - MI ≥ 0.15 & cost ≤ 35%, or MI ≥ 0.25 & cost ≤ 50%
  • Low priority - MI ≥ 0.05 (weak signal or high cost)
  • Skip - MI < 0.05
  • Redundant- known collinear with a baseline feature (from EDA)
Signal is estimated on pairwise-complete rows (rows where both the target and this feature are present), so the MI/correlation numbers are honest - they are not inflated or deflated by imputed values.

Summary - all targets

Each cell shows the recommendation tier for that (feature, target) pair. Features sorted by best tier across all targets.
